Vector.java

00001 /* Vector.java -- Class that provides growable arrays.

00029 modules, and to copy and distribute the resulting executable under
00030 terms of your choice, provided that you also meet, for each linked
00031 independent module, the terms and conditions of the license of that
00032 module.  An independent module is a module which is not derived from
00033 or based on this library.  If you modify this library, you may extend
00034 this exception to your version of the library, but you are not
00035 obligated to do so.  If you do not wish to do so, delete this
00036 exception statement from your version. */
00037 
00038 
00039 package java.util;
00040 
00070 public class Vector extends Object
00071 {
00076     protected Object[] elementData;
00077 
00082     protected int elementCount;
00083 
00090     protected int capacityIncrement;
00091 
00096     public Vector()
00097     {
00098         this(10, 0);
00099     }
00100 
00108     public Vector(int initialCapacity)
00109     {
00110         this(initialCapacity, 0);
00111     }
00112 
00122     public Vector(int initialCapacity, int capacityIncrement)
00123     {
00124         if (initialCapacity < 0)
00125             throw new IllegalArgumentException();
00126 
00127         elementData = new Object[initialCapacity];
00128         this.capacityIncrement = capacityIncrement;
00129     }
00130 
00142     public synchronized void copyInto(Object[] a)
00143     {
00144         System.arraycopy(elementData, 0, a, 0, elementCount);
00145     }
00146 
00152     public synchronized void trimToSize()
00153     {
00154         // Don't bother checking for the case where size() == the capacity of the
00155         // vector since that is a much less likely case; it's more efficient to
00156         // not do the check and lose a bit of performance in that infrequent case
00157 
00158         Object[] newArray = new Object[elementCount];
00159         System.arraycopy(elementData, 0, newArray, 0, elementCount);
00160         elementData = newArray;
00161     }
00162 
00173     public synchronized void ensureCapacity(int minCapacity)
00174     {
00175         if (elementData.length >= minCapacity)
00176             return;
00177 
00178         int newCapacity;
00179 
00180         if (capacityIncrement <= 0)
00181             newCapacity = elementData.length * 2;
00182         else
00183             newCapacity = elementData.length + capacityIncrement;
00184 
00185         Object[] newArray = new Object[Math.max(newCapacity, minCapacity)];
00186 
00187         System.arraycopy(elementData, 0, newArray, 0, elementCount);
00188         elementData = newArray;
00189     }
00190 
00200     public synchronized void setSize(int newSize)
00201     {
00202         // Don't bother checking for the case where size() == the capacity of the
00203         // vector since that is a much less likely case; it's more efficient to
00204         // not do the check and lose a bit of performance in that infrequent case
00205         ensureCapacity(newSize);
00206 
00207         if (newSize < elementCount)
00208         {
00209             for (int i = newSize; i < elementCount; i++)
00210                 elementData[i] = null;
00211         } /* if */
00212 
00213         elementCount = newSize;
00214     }
00215 
00222     public synchronized int capacity()
00223     {
00224         return elementData.length;
00225     }
00226 
00232     public synchronized int size()
00233     {
00234         return elementCount;
00235     }
00236 
00242     public synchronized boolean isEmpty()
00243     {
00244         return elementCount == 0;
00245     }
00246 
00253      // No need to synchronize as the Enumeration is not thread-safe!
00254     public Enumeration elements()
00255     {
00256         return new Enumeration()
00257         {
00258             private int i = 0;
00259 
00260             public boolean hasMoreElements()
00261             {
00262                 return i < elementCount;
00263             }
00264 
00265             public Object nextElement()
00266             {
00267                 if (i >= elementCount)
00268                     throw new NoSuchElementException();
00269 
00270                 return elementData[i++];
00271             }
00272         };
00273     }
00274 
00281     public boolean contains(Object elem)
00282     {
00283         return indexOf(elem, 0) >= 0;
00284     }
00285 
00293     public int indexOf(Object elem)
00294     {
00295         return indexOf(elem, 0);
00296     }
00297 
00308     public synchronized int indexOf(Object e, int index)
00309     {
00310         if (e == null) {
00311             for (int i = index; i < elementCount; i++) {
00312                 if (elementData[i] == null) {
00313                     return i;
00314                 }
00315             }
00316         } else {
00317             for (int i = index; i < elementCount; i++) {
00318                 if (e.equals(elementData[i])) {
00319                     return i;
00320                 }
00321             }
00322         }
00323 
00324         return -1;
00325     }
00326 
00334     public int lastIndexOf(Object elem)
00335     {
00336         return lastIndexOf(elem, elementCount - 1);
00337     }
00338 
00349     public synchronized int lastIndexOf(Object e, int index)
00350         throws IndexOutOfBoundsException
00351     {
00352         checkBoundExclusive(index);
00353 
00354         for (int i = index; i >= 0; i--)
00355             if (e.equals(elementData[i]))
00356                 return i;
00357 
00358         return -1;
00359     }
00360 
00368     public synchronized Object elementAt(int index)
00369         throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException
00370     {
00371         checkBoundExclusive(index);
00372 
00373         return elementData[index];
00374     }
00375 
00382     public synchronized Object firstElement()
00383     {
00384         if (elementCount == 0)
00385             throw new NoSuchElementException();
00386 
00387         return elementData[0];
00388     }
00389 
00396     public synchronized Object lastElement()
00397     {
00398         if (elementCount == 0)
00399             throw new NoSuchElementException();
00400 
00401         return elementData[elementCount - 1];
00402     }
00403 
00411     public void setElementAt(Object obj, int index)
00412     {
00413         set(index, obj);
00414     }
00415 
00423     public void removeElementAt(int index)
00424     {
00425         remove(index);
00426     }
00427 
00436     public synchronized void insertElementAt(Object obj, int index)
00437     {
00438         checkBoundInclusive(index);
00439 
00440         if (elementCount == elementData.length)
00441             ensureCapacity(elementCount + 1);
00442 
00443         System.arraycopy(elementData, index, elementData, index + 1,
00444             elementCount - index);
00445         elementCount++;
00446         elementData[index] = obj;
00447     }
00448 
00455     public synchronized void addElement(Object obj)
00456     {
00457         if (elementCount == elementData.length)
00458             ensureCapacity(elementCount + 1);
00459 
00460         elementData[elementCount++] = obj;
00461     }
00462 
00471     public synchronized boolean removeElement(Object obj)
00472     {
00473         int idx = indexOf(obj, 0);
00474 
00475         if (idx >= 0)
00476         {
00477             remove(idx);
00478             return true;
00479         }
00480 
00481         return false;
00482     }
00483 
00488     public synchronized void removeAllElements()
00489     {
00490         if (elementCount == 0)
00491             return;
00492 
00493         for (int i = 0; i < elementCount; i++)
00494             elementData[i] = null;
00495 
00496         elementCount = 0;
00497     }
00498 
00509     private synchronized Object set(int index, Object element)
00510     {
00511         checkBoundExclusive(index);
00512         Object temp = elementData[index];
00513         elementData[index] = element;
00514         return temp;
00515     }
00516 
00525     private synchronized Object remove(int index)
00526     {
00527         checkBoundExclusive(index);
00528         Object temp = elementData[index];
00529         elementCount--;
00530 
00531         if (index < elementCount)
00532         {
00533             System.arraycopy(elementData, index + 1, elementData, index,
00534                  elementCount - index);
00535         }
00536 
00537         elementData[elementCount] = null;
00538 
00539         return temp;
00540     }
00541 
00548     public synchronized String toString()
00549     {
00550         // Here just for the sychronization.
00551         return super.toString();
00552     }
00553 
00560     private void checkBoundInclusive(int index)
00561     {
00562         // Implementation note: we do not check for negative ranges here, since
00563         // use of a negative index will cause an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException
00564         // with no effort on our part.
00565         if (index > elementCount)
00566             throw new ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException(
00567                 index + " > " + elementCount
00568                 );
00569     }
00570 
00577     private void checkBoundExclusive(int index)
00578     {
00579         // Implementation note: we do not check for negative ranges here, since
00580         // use of a negative index will cause an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException
00581         // with no effort on our part.
00582         if (index >= elementCount)
00583             throw new ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException(
00584                 index + " >= " + elementCount
00585                 );
00586     }
00587 }
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:24 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
